insights: Confirmed snipes are clean authentic autos (Topps Tribute, Authentic Images) with 80%+ price deltas; rejections frequently show facsimile/printed signatures.
16 of 20 rejections (80%) marked wrong_market_price, indicating overpriced bulk/incomplete listings vs. underpriced singles in confirmations.
Blocklist phrases like 'multiple cards', 'pick complete set', 'stock photos' appear only in rejections—clear signals of non-snipe bundle listings.
Both confirmations are graded/certified autos; rejections include ungraded, cut autos, and mixed-player lots (wrong_player: 2 cases).
Rejection reason breakdown: wrong_market_price dominates (13/20, 65%); other low-signal reasons (facsimile, reprint, wrong_player) at 7/20 (35%).
Confirmed snipes feature specific card #/set (2023 Topps Tribute #77, 2001 Authentic Images) vs. rejected vague titles ('group of noted', 'RARE REFRACTOR SSP').
All rejections tagged 'Autograph' with facsimile_signature show Merrick Mint, Gold Edition, Leaf Exhibits MSIG—consistent fake-auto blocklist.
Zero overlap between confirmation titles and rejection titles; confirmations avoid 'investment', 'SSP', 'rare', 'novelty', 'rainbow foil' keywords.
